title: Exact sporting-rule regime expert for wet exemption and Monaco-2025
summary: The FIA regulations create hard discontinuities: dry races require two dry specs unless wets/inters were used, and Monaco 2025 adds a mandatory three-set/two-stop structure.
rationale: The FIA regulations create hard discontinuities: dry races require two dry specs unless wets/inters were used, and Monaco 2025 adds a mandatory three-set/two-stop structure. Generic circuit priors blur these rule edges.
implementation_hint: Create explicit regime flags and/or a small gated expert for `wet_rule_exempt`, `dry_rule_still_unsatisfied`, `monaco_2025_forced_extra_stop`, and `remaining_required_stops_min`. Interact them with progress, stint, prior compounds used, and current compound family rather than only using broad race-name priors.
expected_effect: Should improve rows where a stop is strategically or legally forced even if tyre degradation signals are only moderate.
risk: Affected regimes are sparse, so naive global fitting can overfit unless handled as a gated sidecar or regularized interactions.
